title: Fix 90°/270° rotation in TransformToPdfPoint: restore correct cross-d…
author: sevilbk-trimble

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the coordinate transform used when exporting 360 video station/trajectory annotations to PDF, aiming to correct placement on pages rotated 90°/270°.

**Changes:**
- Refactors `TransformToPdfPoint` to precompute de-normalized coordinates and apply rotation-specific mapping.
- Adjusts 90°/270° rotation math to “swap” axes using the precomputed values.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: `directionX/directionY` are documented as normalized *visual* coordinates, but `x`/`y` are always de-normalized using the unrotated `cropBox.Width()`/`cropBox.Height()`. For `e_90`/`e_270` this makes the mapping scale against the wrong physical dimension (and can push `finalX`/`finalY` outside the crop box on non-square pages). Consider computing visual page dimensions by swapping width/height when rotation is 90/270 (similar to `PDFPageProcessor.cs:187-188`), and using those dimensions in the de-normalization / mapping logic.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This rotation-specific coordinate math is easy to regress (especially on non-square pages). The existing coordinate tests only assert a single interior point and won’t catch dimension-swap or boundary issues for `e_90`/`e_270`. Add test cases that cover the corners (0/1 extremes) and a non-square crop box for both rotations so incorrect scaling shows up immediately.
review_comments.author: Copilot

issue_comments.author: AdamCollins
